macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Wall Street Closes Fifth Straight Losing Week Amid US-Iran War Tensions
items.summary: The S&P 500 fell 1.7% to 6,368.85, Dow dropped 793 points to 45,166.64, and Nasdaq sank 2.1% to 20,948.36, marking the worst week since the Iran war began; markets reacted to shifting US-Iran diplomacy headlines with President Trump extending a deadline on Iran's power plants.

items.title: US Equities Suffer Choppy Week as Iran Diplomacy Signals Swing Sentiment
items.summary: S&P 500 NTM P/E multiple compressed to 19.6x from over 23x last fall; energy sector led gains with 9% month-to-date and 36% YTD amid war backdrop, while Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq posted weekly losses of -3.99% to -7.75% YTD.

items.title: Market Pendulum Swings Back to Value Stocks in Early 2026
items.summary: Value stocks, especially high dividend yielders, outperformed growth stocks early in 2026 after a decade of growth dominance, indicating a potential style rotation in equities.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Drops Below $68K Amid Geopolitical Tensions and $14B Options Expiry
items.summary: Bitcoin traded near $67,000 after breaching $68K support due to Middle East tensions, heavy liquidations, and a $14 billion options expiry, with the Fear & Greed Index hitting extreme fear at 13. Exchange inflows of 14,200 BTC signaled elevated selling pressure, though long-term holders remained steadfast.

items.title: Franklin Templeton Partners with Ondo Finance for On-Chain ETF Access
items.summary: Franklin Templeton collaborated with Ondo Finance to tokenize its ETFs, enabling direct access via crypto wallets without traditional brokers. The New York Stock Exchange also partnered with Securitize to explore blockchain-based stock and ETF settlements.

items.title: Bitmine Immersion Launches MAVAN for Institutional ETH Staking
items.summary: Bitmine Immersion Technologies introduced MAVAN, a U.S.-based staking platform for institutional Ether holdings, allowing custom validator parameters. This reflects growing institutional treatment of ETH staking as a bond-like yield strategy, with the Ethereum Foundation also staking 70,000 ETH.

items.title: Litecoin Gains Regulatory Clarity and LitVM Testnet Launch
items.summary: The SEC classified Litecoin as a digital commodity, similar to XLM, boosting confidence amid a $68.2M whale transfer. LitVM Layer 2 testnet launched in March 2026, enabling EVM-compatible smart contracts for DeFi on Litecoin.

items.title: Bitcoin Shows Resilience Near $70K Despite Traditional Market Downturns
items.summary: Bitcoin held near $70,000, decoupling from declines in S&P 500, gold, and silver amid global conflicts, with whale transactions at multi-year lows. Long-term MVRV ratios suggest a favorable accumulation opportunity despite retail bearishness.

items.title: XRP Price Stagnates at $1.40 Despite Wins and ETFs
items.summary: XRP remains stuck at $1.40 despite commodity status, live ETFs, and Ripple victories, held back by three key factors including market sentiment and liquidity issues.

items.title: THORChain Schedules Community Livestream on March 28
items.summary: THORChain announced a live stream on X at 14:00 UTC on March 28 with Matt Carano of Brave The New World to discuss project updates.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: NeurIPS Apologizes for US Sanctions Policy After Chinese Boycott
items.summary: The top AI conference NeurIPS issued a public apology on March 27, 2026, for restricting submissions from certain Chinese institutions due to US sanctions, following boycotts by China's Computer Federation and other groups threatening to delist the event.

items.title: University of Warwick AI Tool Discovers Over 100 New Planets in NASA Data
items.summary: Astronomers at the University of Warwick used a new AI tool to uncover more than 100 previously hidden exoplanets by analyzing NASA data, marking a significant breakthrough in planetary detection.

items.title: US Marines Announce AI Forum and Hackathon at Modern Day Marine 2026
items.summary: The US Marine Corps announced an Artificial Intelligence Forum and Hackathon on March 26, 2026, focused on AI applications in logistics, predictive maintenance, supply chain, and autonomous systems for warfighter enhancement.

items.title: Colleges Adopt AI Faster Than Governance Structures Can Keep Up
items.summary: Higher education institutions are rapidly integrating AI into admissions, curriculum, and operations, outpacing policy development and raising concerns about academic integrity, privacy, and redefinition of teaching.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Sony Raises PS5 Prices Across Regions
items.summary: Sony has announced another price increase for the PlayStation 5 family of systems, sparking discussions on the console market's future and potential PlayStation 6 launch in holiday 2027.

items.title: Ubisoft Reveals AI-Generated Artwork Slipped into Anno 117: Pax Romana
items.summary: Ubisoft admitted that AI-generated placeholder artwork accidentally appeared in promotional materials for Anno 117: Pax Romana.

items.title: Frontier Removes Generative AI Portraits from Jurassic World Evolution 3 After Backlash
items.summary: Frontier Developments pulled AI-generated portraits from Jurassic World Evolution 3 following fan criticism.

items.title: Tencent Launches AI-Powered 3D Asset Tool for Creators
items.summary: Tencent introduced a generative AI tool for creating 3D assets to empower game developers.

items.title: Video Game History Foundation Archives Complete History of Myst
items.summary: The Video Game History Foundation accomplished a major archival effort preserving the full history of the classic game Myst.

items.title: Secret 6 Madrid Workers Criticize Owner Testronic Amid Studio Closure
items.summary: Employees at Secret 6 Madrid accused owner Testronic of attempting to undermine the studio from the start ahead of its impending closure.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Schiit Audio Launches New Desktop Amplifier and Sony Releases Dolby Atmos Soundbar
items.summary: Schiit Audio announced a new desktop amplifier, while Sony unveiled a new Dolby Atmos soundbar, highlighting key hi-fi audio product releases for the week.

items.title: Tech Companies Pledge to Self-Supply Power for Data Centers
items.summary: Major technology firms including Amazon, Google, Meta, Microsoft, Oracle, OpenAI, and xAI signed a federal pledge to provide their own power for AI data centers to minimize impact on consumer electricity costs.

items.title: AI Surge Complicates Big Tech Climate Goals with Rising Emissions and Natural Gas Reliance
items.summary: Tech giants like Google, Microsoft, Amazon, and Meta report significant emissions increases due to data center demands, with natural gas powering over 40% of U.S. data centers amid challenges to 2030 carbon goals.

items.title: Startups Adopt Continuous Data Room Model for Faster Fundraising
items.summary: Top-tier startups are shifting to a 'Continuous Data Room' approach, maintaining ongoing access to cap tables and board minutes beyond traditional fundraising seasons to accelerate deal speeds.

items.title: Escalating Geopolitical Cyber Tensions Target Critical Infrastructure and Semiconductors
items.summary: State-sponsored cyber operations, including China-linked campaigns like Volt Typhoon and Salt Typhoon, continue to hit telecoms, energy, and high-tech sectors like semiconductors amid rising global conflicts.
